name: user
description: Identity composite: an avatar with a name, description, and optional action slot

meta.name: User
meta.description: Identity composite: an avatar with a name, description, and optional action slot

examples.title: Basic
examples.code: <User name="Jane Doe" description="jane@acme.com" avatarProps={{ src: "/jane.jpg", alt: "Jane Doe" }} />

whenToUse: Showing a person with their name and a secondary line (email, role) next to an avatar
Identity rows inside menus, tables, navbars, and account switchers
A compact, labelled identity primitive too granular for a page-section block
whenNotToUse: Just an image with no name/description — use Avatar directly
A full profile card with rich content — compose a Card instead

antiPatterns.good: <User name="Jane Doe" avatarProps={{ src: "/jane.jpg", alt: "Jane Doe" }} />
antiPatterns.why: The name is the accessible identity label; an empty name leaves the avatar unlabelled

related.name: Avatar
related.reason: User wraps Avatar and forwards avatarProps to it

related.name: AvatarGroup
related.reason: Use AvatarGroup to show several identities as an overlapping stack

intent.a11yRationale: The composite is a labelled group; the name provides the accessible identity and the avatar carries its own role="img"/alt — no redundant roles

flexibility.note: Any ReactNode — email, role, or a status line
Name and description colors must resolve to --cascivo-* tokens
